directive: Use per-username rate limiting: pass `keyFn: (req) => req.body?.username` to the existing ratelimit middleware in src/middleware/ratelimit.ts. Ensure body-parser middleware runs before the rate-limit middleware in the login route chain. Configure threshold and cooldown window in config/auth.yml. Return 429 when the per-username key exceeds threshold.
rationale: The project's stated threat is credential stuffing — attackers rotate IPs but target the same account. Per-IP rate limiting does not address this threat. Per-username directly caps attempts against a single account regardless of source IP, which is the correct defense. The existing ratelimit.ts middleware already supports a keyFn parameter, so per-username requires only passing `(req) => req.body.username` as the key — no new tables, no API contract change. Body parsing before rate limit check is a standard middleware ordering adjustment, not a scope violation.
